scope_boundary: Org + a single default project ONLY. No member invites, no project naming beyond the one default project. ui/ only (NOT frontend/).
acceptance_suite: tests/acceptance/org-onboarding/ (own pyproject.toml + venv; run: cd tests/acceptance/org-onboarding && uv run --no-project pytest). NOT run by the refinery --auto gate — RED-by-design.

walking_skeleton.strategy: C (real local / @real_io) — real compose stack (backend + ui-state + Redis + auth-proxy); no in-memory doubles.
walking_skeleton.green_when: S1 + S2 + S3 + S4 are all delivered (the journey is end to end).

slices.title: Backend: created_by column, DEV_NO_ORG DB resolution, drop auto-create
slices.summary: Add a nullable organizations.created_by, stamp it on org create, resolve the dev principal's org from the DB under DEV_NO_ORG (ignoring X-Org-Id), and drop the auto-create of 'My First Project'. This is what makes the empty-org state reachable for the dev principal AND frees first-project creation for the project-context step.
acceptance_criteria: When the dev principal (DEV_NO_ORG=true) with no owned org calls GET /api/orgs/me via the ingress, it returns 404 (X-Org-Id header is ignored; org resolved by created_by == user.id).
When the principal creates an org via POST /api/orgs, the organizations row persists created_by == user.id (gate-tested backend unit test).
After the org is created, GET /api/orgs/me resolves that org for the same principal under DEV_NO_ORG (200).
When an org is created via POST /api/orgs, NO project is auto-created (GET /api/projects returns []).
DEV_NO_ORG defaults to false; with it off, behaviour is unchanged (header claim honoured).
driving_tests: backend/tests/use_cases/organization/test_create_organization.py (UPDATE: assert zero projects + created_by stamped — keeps the gate green)
files_to_touch: backend/app/repositories/metadata/organization_record.py — add created_by: Mapped[str | None] (nullable).
backend/migrations/versions/018_add_organizations_created_by.py — NEW; add_column nullable String(36); down_revision = 'd7e8f9a0b1c2' (017 head). SQLite + Postgres compatible.
backend/app/repositories/metadata/repository.py — create_organization accepts + persists created_by.
backend/app/use_cases/organization/create_organization.py — pass created_by=user.id; DELETE the create_project('My First Project', ...) auto-create.
backend/app/config.py — add dev_no_org: bool = False (env DEV_NO_ORG).
backend/app/routers/deps.py (or the /api/orgs/me read path with session access) — under dev_no_org + dev principal, ignore X-Org-Id and resolve org_id from the DB by created_by == user.id.
backend/tests/use_cases/organization/test_create_organization.py — update the auto-create assertion (now zero projects); add created_by assertion.
slices.alembic_note: Follow the alembic-migration skill: nullable add_column only; real downgrade (drop_column); no data backfill needed. SQLite dev + Postgres prod both support add nullable column without batch mode.
risks: The DB-touching org resolution must run where an AsyncSession is in context; get_current_user currently has no repo. May need the resolution in the org read use case / a deps helper that depends on use_db_context.

slices.title: ui/ plumbing: port StateProxy (CSR + Bearer) + provider + session_begin bootstrap
slices.summary: Port frontend/app/lib/state-proxy.ts into ui/ adapted to CSR + localStorage Bearer: forward Authorization: Bearer <token> on session_begin, every /ui-state/state/events POST, and GET /state(+/stream). Wrap it in a StateProxyProvider created once at app entry. On authenticated entry, POST {type: session_begin} and await settle. Defensively adopt X-New-Access-Token → setToken (D5, WorkOS-only; not e2e-gated).
acceptance_criteria: createStateProxy in ui/ attaches Authorization: Bearer <token> (from tokenStorage.getToken()) to every /ui-state call (unit test with injected fetchImpl asserts the header).
A StateProxyProvider context exposes a single proxy instance shared across routes (the gate + /onboarding read the same proxy).
On authenticated entry the app POSTs session_begin exactly once and the resulting document is observable via useSelector.
When a /ui-state response carries X-New-Access-Token, the proxy calls setToken with it (WorkOS defensive path).
driving_tests: ui/app/lib/state-proxy.test.ts (NEW vitest — Bearer header on each transport call; X-New-Access-Token adoption). Vitest is NOT gate-run; keep RED-by-skip if scaffolding before impl.
Exercised end to end by the walking skeleton once S3/S4 land.
files_to_touch: ui/app/lib/state-proxy.ts — NEW (port; CSR + Bearer; inject fetchImpl/eventSourceFactory).
ui/app/lib/StateProxyProvider.tsx — NEW (React context; single instance at app entry).
ui/app/auth/bootstrap.ts (or a new bootstrap hook) — POST session_begin on authenticated entry; await settle.
ui/app/root.tsx (ui/) or app entry — mount StateProxyProvider.
Use createLogger from ui/app/lib/log.ts for all logging (never console.*).
risks: frontend/ proxy uses credentials:'include' (cookie). ui/ must instead send the Bearer header and NOT rely on cookies; SSE (EventSource) cannot set Authorization headers — confirm the ui-state SSE auth strategy for CSR (query param or rely on the POST-settled document; see upstream-issues UI-3).

slices.title: ui/ onboarding: /onboarding route outside the shell + gate redirect + needs_org org form
slices.summary: Add /onboarding as a top-level route OUTSIDE the app-shell layout (sibling of /login, /auth/callback) so it renders without Topbar/Overlays. After session_begin settles, the gate redirects to /onboarding when phase == onboarding (onboarding.state in needs_org|creating_org|error_recoverable); verifying is transient (wait, do not redirect). The needs_org surface renders an org-name form that posts org_form_submitted {org_name} and shows the inline org_validation_error.
acceptance_criteria: When an authenticated empty-org principal enters, after session_begin settles they are redirected to /onboarding (not bounced to /login, not /project/undefined).
/onboarding renders OUTSIDE the app-shell (no Topbar/Overlays) and shows the principal's identity (regions.onboarding.context.user).
Submitting a valid org name posts org_form_submitted {org_name}; on ready the surface advances to the project step (S4).
Submitting an invalid/blank org name keeps the user on the org form and shows the inline org_validation_error; state stays needs_org.
While onboarding.state == verifying the gate waits and does not redirect.
driving_tests: ui/app/routes/onboarding.test.tsx (NEW vitest, optional) — gate redirect + form post; not gate-run.
files_to_touch: ui/app/routes.ts — add route('/onboarding', 'routes/onboarding.tsx') as a top-level sibling of /login + /auth/callback (OUTSIDE app-shell).
ui/app/routes/onboarding.tsx — NEW; needs_org org-name form → org_form_submitted; inline validation; identity header.
ui/app/routes/app-shell.tsx — gate: after session_begin settle, redirect to /onboarding when phase == onboarding; wait on verifying.
Use createLogger for logging.
risks: The existing app-shell gate redirects to /login on missing token and reads fixture catalog; the new gate must compose with session_begin settle without double-redirect loops.

slices.title: ui/ default project: no_projects → create_project_submitted form → app entry
slices.summary: After onboarding ready, when regions.projectContext.state == no_projects, the /onboarding surface drives the default-project form: it posts create_project_submitted carrying the project name. On project_selected, onboarding is complete and the app navigates in. (Use create_project_submitted, which carries the name — NOT create_project_clicked.)
acceptance_criteria: After org creation, when projectContext.state == no_projects, /onboarding shows the default-project form.
Submitting the project name posts create_project_submitted carrying the name; on project_selected exactly one project exists with that name.
On completion (org exists AND default project exists) the app navigates into the workspace (out of /onboarding).
The name reaches the backend correctly (see upstream-issues UI-1 — reconcile the create_project_submitted name field).
files_to_touch: ui/app/routes/onboarding.tsx — add the no_projects default-project form → create_project_submitted; navigate into app on project_selected.
ui/app/routes/app-shell.tsx — when ready + no_projects, keep the user on /onboarding until project_selected, then enter.
RECONCILE: the create_project_submitted payload field carrying the name (UI-1) — align ui/ post shape with what project-context's capturePendingProjectName reads.
Use createLogger for logging.
risks: UI-1: project-context's create_project_submitted handler reads the name from a field named org_name (misnomer). DELIVER must reconcile the wire payload shape so the submitted name is not dropped (→ empty-name project → backend 422).

sequencing: S1 first (unblocks every acceptance scenario by making the empty-org state reachable + dropping auto-create). S2 next (the ui/ transport everything else rides). S3 then S4 stack the onboarding surface; the walking skeleton goes GREEN when S4 lands. S2–S4 are ui/-only and not gate-run; S1 is gate-tested by backend pytest.
gate_strategy: DISTILL's MR touches docs/ + tests/acceptance/ ONLY (no backend/ui source). The refinery --auto gate runs backend ruff+pytest, which stays green (no backend source touched; the acceptance suite is a separate non-collected venv). The acceptance suite is RED-by-design and is run locally/by the agent before DELIVER, not by the queue.
